text: Golden, crispy potato balls are arranged on a plate. A pot of water on the stove holds two submerged potatoes boiling for about 30 minutes. Steam rises as a hand places a potato into the pot, checking doneness with a knife.
A blue pot is emptied into a colander in the sink. Peeled and chopped potatoes are placed into a white bowl. Softened butter is added, followed by a pinch of salt sprinkled on top.

The scene begins with a close-up of spices, highlighting black pepper as "the best spice of the entire world." A hand crushes the pepper with a fork, mixing it with two egg yolks. The mixture is covered and placed in a refrigerator for at least 2 hours. After chilling, the mixture is formed into balls and coated with flour, beaten egg with a pinch of salt, and breadcrumbs. The shaped balls are laid on a plate. Next, they are carefully placed one by one into a pan of hot olive oil, ensuring the oil is hot but not too hot to prevent bursting. Once browned, the balls are removed from the oil.
A white plate with a paper towel holds three potato balls. Text: "Remove and leave on paper towel."
On black, a food logo forms. Text: "LIKE."
"Potato balls: 2 potatoes, 3.5 oz, 2 yolks, flour, egg, breadcrumbs."
